analysis_result: 108 peripheral nerve sheath tumor (PNST) samples were included in the MOESM4 Clinical Data table (the primary Toronto cohort for integrated molecular profiling). Breakdown by Tumor Type: MPNST = 16, Low-Grade MPNST = 3, Atypical_NF = 21, Neurofibroma = 35, Cutaneous_NF = 33 (all five categories are PNST subtypes). All 108 samples have methylation data; 49 also have RNA-seq and 54 have WES.
denominator_used: N = 108; definition: all rows in the 'Clinical Data' sheet of 41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx with a non-null Tumor_ID. The full Clinical Data sheet has 108 data rows (shape 108 x 15), each representing one PNST tumor sample in the primary Toronto cohort used for integrated molecular profiling (methylation array on 108/108, RNA-seq on 49/108, WES on 54/108).
assumptions_made: The entire MOESM4 Clinical Data table represents the Toronto primary cohort for integrated molecular profiling (no external/validation cohort samples are mixed in); all five Tumor Type categories (MPNST, Low-Grade MPNST, Atypical_NF, Neurofibroma, Cutaneous_NF) qualify as peripheral nerve sheath tumors (PNSTs); rows with non-null Tumor_ID represent the full set of samples (one row per sample); the Methylation column marked 'X' for all 108 rows indicates every sample in the cohort was profiled on the primary integrative platform (methylation array), consistent with this being the core cohort for integrated profiling.
step_by_step_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - File: /data/41467_2023_38432_MOESM4_ESM.xlsx, sheet 'Clinical Data'
   - Shape after reading with header=0: 108 rows x 15 columns
   - Relevant columns: Tumor_ID, Tumor Type, Methylation_Group, Location, Methylation, RNA-Seq, WES

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Filter 1: rows with non-null Tumor_ID -> 108 samples (all rows satisfy this)
   - Final analytic cohort: N = 108 PNST samples (Toronto primary cohort)

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Tumor Type categorical values (all are PNST subtypes):
     MPNST            = 16 (malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor)
     Low-Grade MPNST  = 3
     Atypical_NF      = 21 (atypical neurofibromatous neoplasm of uncertain biological potential)
     Neurofibroma     = 35 (plexiform/benign)
     Cutaneous_NF     = 33 (cutaneous neurofibroma)
     Total            = 108
   - Methylation_Group: 7 subgroups (MPNST_G1=8, MPNST_G2=8, PremalignantNF_G3=29, Benign_NF-G4=20, Sporadic Spinal NF_G5=10, Cutaneous NF_G6=12, Cutaneous NF_G7=21; sum = 108)
   - Location: Peripheral=46, Cutaneous=33, Spinal=29; sum = 108
   - Sample ID prefixes (NF=56, CN=29, M=19, cNF=4) consistent with neurofibroma/cutaneous-NF/MPNST annotations

D. COMPUTATION
   - Numerator: 108 samples in MOESM4 Clinical Data with non-null Tumor_ID
   - Denominator: not a proportion; this is a simple count of rows representing unique PNST samples in the primary Toronto cohort table
   - All 108 samples have Methylation='X' (the primary integrated profiling modality); 49 have RNA-Seq='X' and 54 have WES='X'

E. RESULT
   - 108 PNST samples in the MOESM4 Clinical Data table (primary Toronto cohort for integrated molecular profiling)

F. CAVEATS
   - The question asks specifically about the count in the MOESM4 Clinical Data table; that table lists 108 samples. This is consistent with the data context note ('108 samples + header').
   - All tumor types in the table are PNST subtypes (benign/premalignant neurofibromas, atypical NFs, low-grade and high-grade MPNSTs, cutaneous NFs), so no filter on Tumor Type is needed to restrict to PNSTs.
   - Not every sample has every molecular modality: 108 methylation, 49 RNA-seq, 54 WES. The 108 count reflects the total integrated-profiling cohort where at least methylation (the unifying platform for integrated methylation-based classification) was performed on all samples.
   - No missing data in Tumor_ID or Tumor Type columns.
